title: Tabernaemontana retusa
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana retusa belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes a diverse range of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of evergreen shrubs or small trees found in various parts of the world, particularly in the tropics. Within the genus, Tabernaemontana retusa is classified as a species of flowering plant, characterized by its distinctive appearance and growth habits. The classification of Tabernaemontana retusa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Apocynaceae family.

side_effects: Tabernaemontana retusa, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, but excessive consumption can cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. intro: Tabernaemontana retusa, also known as the Philippine yellow tabernaemontana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, as well as the ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana retusa include alkaloids such as tabernanthine, voacangine, and voacristine, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
